name: general-superstaq

metadata.name: general-superstaq
metadata.summary: The general module that provides tools and access to Superstaq.

documentation:
# general-superstaq
This package contains code that is common across our clients ([cirq-superstaq](https://github.com/Infleqtion/client-superstaq#-cirq-superstaq-) and [qiskit-superstaq](https://github.com/Infleqtion/client-superstaq#-qiskit-superstaq-)) and [Supermarq](https://github.com/Infleqtion/client-superstaq#-supermarq-).

classes.name: general_superstaq.typing.Job
documentation: A class to store data for a Superstaq job.

classes.name: general_superstaq.typing.Target
documentation: A data class to store data returned from a `/get_targets` request.

modules.name: general_superstaq.serialization_test
documentation: pylint: disable=missing-function-docstring,missing-class-docstring

functions.name: general_superstaq.validation.validate_integer_param
documentation:
Validates that `integer_param` is an integer and positive (or above a minimum value).

Args:
    integer_param: The input parameter to validate.
    min_val: Optional parameter to validate if `integer_param` is greater than `min_val`.

Raises:
    TypeError: If `integer_param` is not an integer.
    ValueError: If `integer_param` is less than `min_val`.

functions.name: general_superstaq.validation.validate_target
documentation:
Checks that `target` conforms to a valid Superstaq format and device type.

Args:
    target: A string containing the name of a target device.

Raises:
    ValueError: If `target` has an invalid format or device type.

functions.name: general_superstaq.validation.validate_noise_type
documentation:
Validates that an ACES noise model is valid.

Args:
    noise: A noise model parameter.
    n_qubits: Number of qubits the noise model is applied to.

Raises:
    ValueError: If `noise` is not valid.

functions.name: general_superstaq.validation.validate_qubo
documentation:
Validates that the input can be converted into a valid QUBO.

Args:
    qubo: The input value to validate.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the provided object cannot be converted into a valid QUBO.

classes.name: general_superstaq.service.Service

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the `Service` class.

Args:
    client: The Superstaq client to use.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.get_balance
documentation:
Get the querying user's account balance in USD.

Args:
    pretty_output: Whether to return a pretty string or a float of the balance.

Returns:
    If `pretty_output` is `True`, returns the balance as a nicely formatted string
    ($-prefix, commas on LHS every three digits, and two digits after period).
    Otherwise, simply returns a float of the balance.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service._accept_terms_of_use
documentation:
Send acceptance of terms of use at https://superstaq.infleqtion.com/terms_of_use.

Args:
    user_input: If "YES", server will mark user as having accepted terms of use.

Returns:
    A string message indicating if user has been marked as having accepted terms of use.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.add_new_user
documentation:
Adds new user.

Args:
    name: The name to add.
    email: The new user's email.

Returns:
    String containing status of update (whether or not it failed) and the new user's token.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.update_user_balance
documentation:
Updates user's balance.

Args:
    email: The new user's email.
    balance: The new balance.

Raises:
    superstaq.SuperstaqException: If requested balance exceeds the limit.

Returns:
    String containing status of update (whether or not it failed).

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.update_user_role
documentation:
Updates user's role.

Args:
    email: The new user's email.
    role: The new role.

Returns:
    String containing status of update (whether or not it failed).

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.get_targets
documentation:
Gets a list of Superstaq targets along with their status information.

Args:
    simulator: Optional flag to restrict the list of targets to (non-) simulators.
    supports_submit: Optional boolean flag to only return targets that (don't) allow
        circuit submissions.
    supports_submit_qubo: Optional boolean flag to only return targets that (don't)
        allow qubo submissions.
    supports_compile: Optional boolean flag to return targets that (don't) support
        circuit compilation.
    available: Optional boolean flag to only return targets that are (not) available
        to use.
    retired: Optional boolean flag to only return targets that are or are not retired.
    kwargs: Any additional, supported flags to restrict/filter returned targets.

Returns:
    A list of Superstaq targets matching all provided criteria.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.get_my_targets
documentation:
Gets a list of Superstaq targets that the user can submit to and are available along
with their status information.

Returns:
    A list of Superstaq targets that the user can currently submit to.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.get_user_info

returns.name: dict[str, str | float] | list[dict[str, str | float]]

documentation:
Gets a dictionary of the user's info.

:::{note}
SUPERTECH users can submit optional {code}`name` and/or {code}`email` keyword only
arguments which can be used to search for the info of arbitrary users on the server.
:::

Args:

: name: A name to search by. Defaults to None.
  email: An email address to search by. Defaults to None.
  user_id: A user ID to search by. Defaults to None.

Returns:

: A dictionary of the user information. In the case that either the name or email
  query kwarg is used, a list of dictionaries is returned, corresponding to the user
  information for each user that matches the query.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.submit_qubo
documentation:
Solves a submitted QUBO problem via annealing.

This method returns any number of specified dictionaries that seek the minimum of
the energy landscape from the given objective function known as output solutions.

Args:
    qubo: A dictionary representing the QUBO object. The tuple keys represent the
        boolean variables of the QUBO and the values represent the coefficients.
        As an example, for a QUBO with integer coefficients = 2*a + a*b - 5*b*c - 3
        (where a, b, and c are boolean variables), the corresponding dictionary format
        would be {('a',): 2, ('a', 'b'): 1, ('b', 'c'): -5, (): -3}.
    target: The target to submit the QUBO.
    repetitions: Number of times that the execution is repeated before stopping.
    method: The parameter specifying method of QUBO solving execution. Currently,
        will either be the "dry-run" option which runs on dwave's simulated annealer,
        or defaults to `None` and sends it directly to the specified target.
    max_solutions: A parameter that specifies the max number of output solutions.

Returns:
    A dictionary containing the output solutions.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.aqt_upload_configs
documentation:
Uploads configs for AQT.

Arguments can be either file paths (in .yaml format) or qtrl Manager instances.

Args:
    pulses: `PulseManager` or file path for pulse configuration.
    variables: `VariableManager` or file path for variable configuration.

Returns:
    A status of the update (whether or not it failed).

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.aqt_get_configs
documentation:
Retrieves the raw AQT config files that had previously been uploaded to Superstaq.

Returns:
    A dictionary containing all of the user's configs (as YAML strings), indexed by the
    config names (e.g. "pulses", "variables").

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.aqt_download_configs
documentation:
Downloads AQT configs that had previously been uploaded to Superstaq.

Optionally saves configs to disk as YAML configuration files. Otherwise, the PyYAML package
is required to read the downloaded configs.

Args:
    pulses_file_path: Where to write the pulse configuration.
    variables_file_path: Where to write the variable configuration.
    overwrite: Whether or not to overwrite existing files.

Returns:
    (If file paths are not provided) a tuple of pulses (a dictionary containing pulse
    configuration data) and variables (a dictionary containing calibration variables).

Raises:
    ValueError: If either file path already exists and overwrite is not True.
    ModuleNotFoundError: If file paths are unspecified and PyYAML cannot be imported.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.submit_aces
documentation:
Submits the jobs to characterize `target` through the ACES protocol.

The following gate eigenvalues are eestimated. For each qubit in the device, we consider
six Clifford gates. These are given by the XZ maps: XZ, ZX, -YZ, -XY, ZY, YX. For each of
these gates, three eigenvalues are returned (X, Y, Z, in that order). Then, the two-qubit
gate considered here is the CZ in linear connectivity (each qubit n with n + 1). For this
gate, 15 eigenvalues are considered: XX, XY, XZ, XI, YX, YY, YZ, YI, ZX, ZY, ZZ, ZI, IX, IY
IZ, in that order.

If n qubits are characterized, the first 18 * n entries of the list returned by
`process_aces` will contain the  single-qubit eigenvalues for each gate in the order above.
After all the single-qubit eigenvalues, the next 15 * (n - 1) entries will contain for the
CZ connections, in ascending order.

The protocol in detail can be found in: https://arxiv.org/abs/2108.05803.

Args:
    target: The device target to characterize.
    qubits: A list with the qubit indices to characterize.
    shots: How many shots to use per circuit submitted.
    num_circuits: How many random circuits to use in the protocol.
    mirror_depth: The half-depth of the mirror portion of the random circuits.
    extra_depth: The depth of the fully random portion of the random circuits.
    method: Which type of method to execute the circuits with.
    noise: Noise model to simulate the protocol with. Valid strings are
        "symmetric_depolarize", "phase_flip", "bit_flip" and "asymmetric_depolarize".
    error_prob: The error probabilities if a string was passed to `noise`.
        * For "asymmetric_depolarize", `error_prob` will be a three-tuple with the error
        rates for the X, Y, Z gates in that order. So, a valid argument would be
        `error_prob = (0.1, 0.1, 0.1)`. Notice that these values must add up to less than
        or equal to 1.
        * For the other channels, `error_prob` is one number less than or equal to 1, e.g.,
        `error_prob = 0.1`.
    tag: Tag for all jobs submitted for this protocol.
    lifespan: How long to store the jobs submitted for in days (only works with right
        permissions).

Returns:
    A string with the job id for the ACES job created.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the target or noise model are not valid.
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If the request fails.

methods.name: general_superstaq.service.Service.process_aces
documentation:
Process a job submitted through `submit_aces`.

Args:
    job_id: The job id returned by `submit_aces`.

Returns:
    The estimated eigenvalues.
This class contains all the services that are used to operate Superstaq.

A class to store data returned from a /resource_estimate request.

documentation: Exceptions for the Superstaq API.

classes.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_exceptions.SuperstaqException

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_exceptions.SuperstaqException.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the `SupertaqException` class.

Args:
    message: A message corresponding to the HTTP response status code. Defaults to None.
An exception for errors coming from Superstaq's API.

classes.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_exceptions.SuperstaqUnsuccessfulJobException

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_exceptions.SuperstaqUnsuccessfulJobException.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the `SuperstaqUnsuccessfulJobException` class.

Args:
    job_id: The job identifier of the unsuccessful job.
    status: The status of the unsuccessful job.

An exception for attempting to get info about an unsuccessful job.

This exception occurs when a job has been cancelled, deleted, or failed, and information about
this job is attempted to be accessed.

classes.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_exceptions.SuperstaqServerException

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_exceptions.SuperstaqServerException.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the `SuperstaqServerException` class.

Args:
    message: The message to be displayed for this exception.
    status_code: An HTTP status code, if coming from an HTTP response with a failing
        status.
    contact_info: Whether or not to display contact information.
An exception for non-retriable server-side errors.

documentation: A warning issued by the server.

functions.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client.find_api_key
documentation:
Function to try to load a Superstaq API key from the environment or a key file.

Raises:
    OSError: If the Superstaq API key could not be found in the environment.
    EnvironmentError: If the Superstaq API key could not be found.

Returns:
    Superstaq API key string.

classes.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.__init__
documentation:
Creates the SuperstaqClient.

Users should use `$client_superstaq.Service` instead of this class directly.

The SuperstaqClient handles making requests to the SuperstaqClient,
returning dictionary results. It handles retry and authentication.

Args:
    client_name: The name of the client.
    api_key: The key used for authenticating against the Superstaq API.
    remote_host: The url of the server exposing the Superstaq API. This will strip anything
        besides the base scheme and netloc, i.e. it only takes the part of the host of
        the form `http://example.com` of `http://example.com/test`.
    api_version: Which version fo the api to use, defaults to client_superstaq.API_VERSION,
        which is the most recent version when this client was downloaded.
    max_retry_seconds: The time to continue retriable responses. Defaults to 3600.
    verbose: Whether to print to stderr and stdio any retriable errors that are encountered.
    cq_token: Token from CQ cloud. This is required to submit circuits to CQ hardware.
    ibmq_token: Your IBM Quantum or IBM Cloud token. This is required to submit circuits
        to IBM hardware, or to access non-public IBM devices you may have access to.
    ibmq_instance: An optional instance to use when running IBM jobs.
    ibmq_channel: The type of IBM account. Must be either "ibm_quantum" or "ibm_cloud".
    kwargs: Other optimization and execution parameters.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.get_superstaq_version
documentation:
Gets Superstaq version from response header.

Returns:
    A `dict` containing the current Superstaq version.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.create_job
documentation:
Create a job.

Args:
    serialized_circuits: The serialized representation of the circuit to run.
    repetitions: The number of times to repeat the circuit. For simulation the repeated
        sampling is not done on the server, but is passed as metadata to be recovered
        from the returned job.
    target: Target to run on.
    method: Which type of method to execute the circuits (noisy simulator,
    non-noisy simulator, hardware, e.t.c)
    kwargs: Other optimization and execution parameters.

Returns:
    The json body of the response as a dict. This does not contain populated information
    about the job, but does contain the job id.

Raises:
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: if the request fails.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.cancel_jobs
documentation:
Cancel jobs associated with given job ids.

Args:
    job_ids: The UUIDs of the jobs (returned when the jobs were created).
    kwargs: Extra options needed to fetch jobs.

Returns:
    A list of the job ids of the jobs that successfully cancelled.

Raises:
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: For other API call failures.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.fetch_jobs
documentation:
Get the job from the Superstaq API.

Args:
    job_ids: The UUIDs of the jobs (returned when the jobs were created).
    kwargs:  Extra options needed to fetch jobs.

Returns:
    The json body of the response as a dict.

Raises:
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: For other API call failures.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.get_balance
documentation:
Get the querying user's account balance in USD.

Returns:
    The json body of the response as a dict.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.get_user_info
documentation:
Gets a dictionary of the user's info.

:::{note}
SUPERTECH users can submit optional {code}`name` or {code}`email`
arguments which can be used to search for the info of arbitrary users on the server.
:::

Args:

: name: A name to search by. Defaults to None.
  email: An email address to search by. Defaults to None.
  user_id: A user ID to search by. Defaults to None.

Returns:

: A list of dictionaries corresponding to the user
  information for each user that matches the query. If no {code}`name` or {code}`email`
  parameters are used this dictionary will have length 1.

Raises:

: \~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If the server returns an empty response.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient._accept_terms_of_use
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to confirm acceptance of terms of use.

Args:
    user_input: The user's response to prompt for acceptance of TOU. Server accepts YES.

Returns:
    String with success message.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.get_targets
documentation:
Makes a GET request to retrieve targets from the Superstaq API.

Args:
    kwargs: Optional flags to restrict/filter returned targets.

Returns:
    A list of Superstaq targets matching all provided criteria.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.get_my_targets
documentation:
Makes a GET request to retrieve targets from the Superstaq API.

Returns:
    A list of Superstaq targets matching all provided criteria.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.add_new_user
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to add a new user.

Args:
    json_dict: The dictionary with user entry.

Returns:
    The response as a string.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.update_user_balance
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to update a user's balance in the database.

Args:
    json_dict: The dictionary with user entry and new balance.

Returns:
    The response as a string.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.update_user_role
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to update a user's role.

Args:
    json_dict: The dictionary with user entry and new role.

Returns:
    The response as a string.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.resource_estimate
documentation:
POSTs the given payload to the `/resource_estimate` endpoint.

Args:
    json_dict: The payload to POST.

Returns:
    The response of the given payload.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.aqt_compile
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to compile a list of circuits for Berkeley-AQT.

Args:
    json_dict: The dictionary containing data to compile.

Returns:
    A dictionary containing compiled circuit(s) data.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.qscout_compile
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to compile a list of circuits for QSCOUT.

Args:
    json_dict: The dictionary containing data to compile.

Returns:
    A dictionary containing compiled circuit(s) data.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.compile
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to compile a list of circuits.

Args:
    json_dict: The dictionary containing data to compile.

Returns:
    A dictionary containing compiled circuit data.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.submit_qubo
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to submit a QUBO problem to the
given target.

Args:
    qubo: A dictionary representing the QUBO object. The tuple keys represent the
        boolean variables of the QUBO and the values represent the coefficients.
        As an example, for a QUBO with integer coefficients = 2*a + a*b - 5*b*c - 3
        (where a, b, and c are boolean variables), the corresponding dictionary format
        would be {('a',): 2, ('a', 'b'): 1, ('b', 'c'): -5, (): -3}.
    target: The target to submit the QUBO.
    repetitions: Number of times that the execution is repeated before stopping.
    method: The parameter specifying method of QUBO solving execution. Currently,
        will either be the "dry-run" option which runs on dwave's simulated annealer,
        or defaults to `None` and sends it directly to the specified target.
    max_solutions: A parameter that specifies the max number of output solutions.

Returns:
    A dictionary from the POST request.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.supercheq
documentation:
Performs a POST request on the `/supercheq` endpoint.

Args:
    files: List of files specified as binary using integers.
        For example: [[1, 0, 1], [1, 1, 1]].
    num_qubits: Number of qubits to run Supercheq on.
    depth: The depth of the circuits to run Supercheq on.
    circuit_return_type: Supports only `cirq` and `qiskit` for now.

Returns:
    The output of Supercheq.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.submit_dfe
documentation:
Performs a POST request on the `/dfe_post` endpoint.

Args:
    circuit_1: Serialized circuit that prepares the first state for the protocol.
    target_1: Target to prepare the first state on.
    circuit_2: Serialized circuit that prepares the second state for the protocol.
    target_2: Target to prepare the second state on.
    num_random_bases: Number of random bases to measure the states on.
    shots: Number of shots per random basis.
    kwargs: Other execution parameters.
        - tag: Tag for all jobs submitted for this protocol.
        - lifespan: How long to store the jobs submitted for in days (only works with right
        permissions).
        - method: Which type of method to execute the circuits with.

Returns:
    A list of size two with the ids for the RMT jobs created; these ids should be passed to
    `process_dfe` to get back the fidelity estimation.

Raises:
    ValueError: If any of the targets passed are not valid.
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: if the request fails.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.process_dfe
documentation:
Performs a POST request on the `/dfe_fetch` endpoint.

Args:
    job_ids: A list of job ids returned by a call to `submit_dfe`.

Returns:
    The estimated fidelity between the two states as a float.

Raises:
    ValueError: If `job_ids` is not of size two.
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If the request fails.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.submit_aces
documentation:
Performs a POST request on the `/aces` endpoint.

Args:
    target: The device target to characterize.
    qubits: A list with the qubit indices to characterize.
    shots: How many shots to use per circuit submitted.
    num_circuits: How many random circuits to use in the protocol.
    mirror_depth: The half-depth of the mirror portion of the random circuits.
    extra_depth: The depth of the fully random portion of the random circuits.
    method: Which type of method to execute the circuits with.
    noise: A dictionary describing a noise model to simulate the run with.
    tag: Tag for all jobs submitted for this protocol.
    lifespan: How long to store the jobs submitted for in days (only works with right
        permissions).
    weights: The weights of the Pauli strings.
Returns:
    A string with the job id for the ACES job created.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the target or noise model is not valid.
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If the request fails.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.process_aces
documentation:
Makes a POST request to the "/aces_fetch" endpoint.

Args:
    job_id: The job id returned by `submit_aces`.

Returns:
    The estimated eigenvalues.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.submit_cb
documentation:
Makes a POST request to the `/cycle_benchmarking` endpoint.

Args:
    target: The target device to characterize.
    shots: How many shots to use per circuit submitted.
    serialized_circuits: The serialized process circuits to use in the protocol.
    n_channels: The number of random Pauli decay channels to approximate error.
    n_sequences: Number of circuits to generate per depth.
    depths: Lists of depths representing the depths of Cycle Benchmarking circuits
        to generate.
    method: Optional method to use in device submission (e.g. "dry-run").
    noise: Dictionary representing noise model to simulate the protocol with.

Returns:
    A string with the job id for the Cycle Benchmarking job created.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the target or noise model is not valid.
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If the request fails.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.process_cb
documentation:
Makes a POST request to the "/cb_fetch" endpoint.

Args:
    job_id: The job id returned by `submit_cb`.
    counts: Optional dict representing result counts.

Returns:
    Characterization data including process fidelity
    and parameter estimates.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.target_info
documentation:
Makes a POST request to the /target_info endpoint.

Uses the Superstaq API to request information about `target`.

Args:
    target: A string representing the device to get information about.

Returns:
    The target information.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.aqt_upload_configs
documentation:
Makes a POST request to Superstaq API to upload configurations.

Args:
    aqt_configs: The configs to be uploaded.

Returns:
    A string response from POST request.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.aqt_get_configs
documentation:
Writes AQT configs from the AQT system onto the given file path.

Returns:
    A dictionary containing the AQT configs.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.get_request
documentation:
Performs a GET request on a given endpoint.

Args:
    endpoint: The endpoint to perform the GET request on.
    query: An optional query dictionary to include in the get request.
        This query will be appended to the url.

Returns:
    The response of the GET request.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient.post_request
documentation:
Performs a POST request on a given endpoint with a given payload.

Args:
    endpoint: The endpoint to perform the POST request on.
    json_dict: The payload to POST.

Returns:
    The response of the POST request.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient._handle_status_codes
documentation:
A method to handle status codes.

Args:
    response: The `requests.Response` to get the status codes from.

Raises:
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If unauthorized by Superstaq API.
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If an error has occurred in making a request
        to the Superstaq API.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient._prompt_accept_terms_of_use
documentation:
Prompts terms of use.

Raises:
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If terms of use are not accepted.

methods.name: general_superstaq.superstaq_client._SuperstaqClient._make_request
documentation:
Make a request to the API, retrying if necessary.

Args:
    request: A function that returns a `requests.Response`.

Raises:
    ~gss.SuperstaqServerException: If there was a not-retriable error from
        the API.
    TimeoutError: If the requests retried for more than `max_retry_seconds`.

Returns:
    The `requests.Response` from the final successful request call.

Handles calls to Superstaq's API.

Users should not instantiate this themselves,
but instead should use `$client_superstaq.Service`.

functions.name: general_superstaq.serialization.bytes_to_str
documentation:
Convert arbitrary bytes data into a string.

Args:
    bytes_data: The data to be converted.

Returns:
    The string from conversion.

functions.name: general_superstaq.serialization.str_to_bytes
documentation:
Decode the string-encoded bytes data returned by `bytes_to_str`.

Args:
    str_data: The string data to be decoded.

Returns:
    The decoded by bytes data.

functions.name: general_superstaq.serialization.serialize
documentation:
Serialize picklable object into a string.

Args:
    obj: A picklable object to be serialized.

Returns:
    The string representing the serialized object.

functions.name: general_superstaq.serialization.deserialize
documentation:
Deserialize serialized objects.

Args:
    serialized_obj: A string generated via `general_superstaq.serialization.serialize`.

Returns:
    The serialized object.

modules.name: general_superstaq._models
documentation: Data models used when communicating with the superstaq server.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.JobType

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobType.DEVICE_SUBMISSION
documentation: A job that involves submitting circuits to live quantum hardware (device).

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobType.DEVICE_SIMULATION
documentation:
A job that involves simulating a circuit as if it was on some live hardware (device).
May or may not include noise (depending on options dict).

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobType.SIMULATION
documentation: Simulation of a given quantum circuit but agnostic to any specific device.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobType.COMPILE
documentation: A job that only involves compiling a circuit for some given device.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobType.CONVERT
documentation: A job that only involves converting circuits between e.g. qiskit and cirq
The different types of jobs that can be submitted through Superstaq.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.SimMethod

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.SimMethod.STATEVECTOR
documentation: Simulation calculates the wavefunction of the state at the end of the circuit.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.SimMethod.NOISE_SIM
documentation:
The simulation applies noise. If used with `device_simulation` this applies realistic device
noise otherwise the noise needs to be provided by the user (via the options dict).

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.SimMethod.SIM
documentation: Samples the circuits without any noise.
The different simulation methods that are available.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitType
documentation: The different languages that are recognized by Superstaq.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.RECEIVED
documentation: The job has been received (and accepted) to the server and is awaiting further action.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.AWAITING_COMPILE
documentation: The job is waiting for a worker to compile.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.AWAITING_SUBMISSION
documentation: The job is waiting for a worker to submit the circuit to an external device.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.AWAITING_SIMULATION
documentation: The job is waiting for a worker to simulate.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.AWAITING_CONVERSION
documentation: The job is waiting for a worker to convert.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.RUNNING
documentation: The job is being run by a worker.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.COMPLETED
documentation: The job is completed.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.FAILED
documentation: The job failed. A reason should be stored in the job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.CANCELLED
documentation: The job was cancelled. A reason should be stored in the job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.UNRECOGNIZED
documentation: Something has gone wrong! (Treated as terminal)

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.PENDING
documentation:
When a job has been submitted to an external provider but that provider has
not yet run the job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.CircuitStatus.DELETED
documentation: When a job has been deleted.
The possible statuses of a job/circuit.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.DefaultPydanticModel
documentation: Default pydantic model used across the superstaq server.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.job_type
documentation: The type of job being submitted.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.statuses
documentation: The current status of each circuit in the job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.status_messages
documentation: Any status messages for each circuit in the job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.user_email
documentation: The email address of the use who submitted the job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.target
documentation: The target that the job was submitted to.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.provider_id
documentation: Any provider side ID's for each circuit in the job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.num_circuits
documentation: Number of circuits in the job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.compiled_circuit_type
documentation: The compiled circuit type.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.compiled_circuits
documentation: Compiled versions of each input circuits.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.input_circuits
documentation: The input circuits as serialized strings.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.input_circuit_type
documentation: The input circuit type.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.pulse_gate_circuits
documentation: Serialized pulse gate circuits (if relevant).

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.counts
documentation: Counts for each input circuit (if available/relevant).

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.state_vectors
documentation: State vector results for each input circuit (if available/relevant).

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.results_dicts
documentation: Serialized results dictionary for each input circuit (if available/relevant).

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.num_qubits
documentation: Number of qubits required for each circuit.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.shots
documentation: Number of shots for each circuit.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.dry_run
documentation: Flag to indicate a dry-run job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.submission_timestamp
documentation: Timestamp when the job was submitted.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.last_updated_timestamp
documentation: Timestamp for when each circuit was last updated.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.initial_logical_to_physicals
documentation: Serialized initial logical-to-physical mapping for each circuit.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobData.final_logical_to_physicals
documentation: Serialized initial final-to-physical mapping for each circuit.
A class to store data for a Superstaq job which is returned through to the client.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.job_type
documentation: The job type.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.target
documentation: The target.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.circuits
documentation: Serialized input circuits.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.circuit_type
documentation: The input circuit type.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.verbatim
documentation: Whether to skip compile step.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.compiled_circuit_type
documentation:
The desired circuit type for the compiled circuits. Used mainly for `convert` jobs. If
None then the input circuit type is assumed.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.shots
documentation: Number of shots.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.dry_run
documentation: Flag for a dry-run

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.sim_method
documentation: The simulation method to use. Only used on `simulation` jobs.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.priority
documentation:
Optional priority level. Note that different roles have their own maximum priority level
which will limit the priority that users can submit.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.options_dict
documentation: Options dictionary with additional configuration detail.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJob.tags
documentation: Optional tags.
The data model for submitting new jobs.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.JobCancellationResults

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobCancellationResults.succeeded
documentation: List of circuits that successfully cancelled.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobCancellationResults.message
documentation: The server message.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobCancellationResults.warnings
documentation: Any warnings generated when cancelling.
The results from cancelling a job.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJobResponse

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJobResponse.job_id
documentation: The job ID for the submitted job.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.NewJobResponse.num_circuits
documentation: The number of circuits in the job.
Model for the response when a new job is submitted

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery.user_email
documentation: List of user emails to include.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery.job_id
documentation: List of job IDs to include.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery.target_name
documentation: List of targets to include.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery.status
documentation: List of statuses to include.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery.min_priority
documentation: Minimum priority to include.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery.max_priority
documentation: Maximum priority to include.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery.submitted_before
documentation: Filter for jobs submitted before this date.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.JobQuery.submitted_after
documentation: Filter for jobs submitted after this date.

The query model for retrieving jobs. Using multiple values in a field is interpreted as
logical OR while providing values for multiple fields is interpreted as logical AND.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.UserTokenResponse

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserTokenResponse.email
documentation: The user's email address.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserTokenResponse.token
documentation: The user's new token.

Model for returning a user token to the client, either when adding a new user or
regenerating the token.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.BalanceResponse

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.BalanceResponse.email
documentation: The user's email address.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.BalanceResponse.balance
documentation: The user's balance.
Model for returning a single user balance.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.UserInfo

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserInfo.name
documentation: User name.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserInfo.email
documentation: User email.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserInfo.role
documentation: User role.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserInfo.balance
documentation: User balance

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserInfo.token
documentation: User API token.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserInfo.user_id
documentation: User id
Model for the user info returned to the client.

classes.name: general_superstaq._models.UserQuery

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserQuery.name
documentation: List of user names to filter for.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserQuery.email
documentation: List of user emails to filter for.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserQuery.role
documentation: List of user roles to filter for.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.UserQuery.user_id
documentation: List of explicit user IDs to filter for.

Model for querying the database to retrieve users. Use of lists implied logical OR. Providing
multiple fields (e.g. name and email) implies logical AND.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.GetTargetsFilterModel.supports_submit

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.GetTargetsFilterModel.supports_submit_qubo
documentation: Include Superstaq targets that allow/do not allow QUBO submission.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.GetTargetsFilterModel.supports_compile
documentation: Include Superstaq targets that allow/do not allow circuit compilation.

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.GetTargetsFilterModel.available

class_variables.name: general_superstaq._models.GetTargetsFilterModel.retired
